datadog.name: Datadog

review_excerpts: Datadog has become our go-to observability platform. The APM traces are excellent, dashboards are flexible, and the Kubernetes integration saved us weeks of setup.
Pricing creep is the main pain. Every feature is a separate SKU. We tripled our bill in 18 months as we added more hosts and custom metrics.
Incredibly powerful for a team that can invest in setup. The learning curve is steep but worth it for production-grade observability at scale.
The UI is genuinely best-in-class. Dashboards feel like a product that engineers actually use, not an ops afterthought.
Support is slow for non-enterprise tiers. Took 4 days to get a response on a billing question. The product itself is solid though.

recent_incidents: Resolved - Feb 2024: Elevated error rates on metrics ingestion in us-east-1. Duration: 47 min.
Resolved - Nov 2023: APM trace search degraded performance. Duration: 2h 13min.

funding.name: Datadog
funding.notable: Revenue $2.1B FY2023, 28% YoY growth. ARR growing but sales efficiency declining post-2022.

ae_quote.competitor_dig: We're more reliable than New Relic during incidents.

datadog.trash_talk: We're more reliable than New Relic during incidents.

newrelic.name: New Relic

review_excerpts: New Relic's shift to consumption-based pricing was painful for teams with spiky workloads. Predictability went out the window.
The 100GB free tier is genuinely useful for small teams. Onboarding is fast with the guided install.
Query language (NRQL) is powerful once you learn it. Comparable to Splunk SPL but less steep.
Had a major ingestion incident in Q3 2023. Alerts stopped firing for 90 minutes. That shook our confidence.
Better priced than Datadog for log-heavy workloads. APM traces feel slightly inferior though.

recent_incidents: Resolved - Sep 2023: Alert notifications delayed for ~90 min in US region.
Resolved - Jan 2024: Slow log query response times, degraded for 30 min.

funding.name: New Relic
funding.status: Private (taken private by Francisco Partners + TPG, 2023)
funding.notable: Went private Nov 2023 after struggling public valuation. New ownership likely to push aggressive discounting to win market share back from Datadog.

ae_quote.competitor_dig: Datadog will nickel-and-dime you on every custom metric.

newrelic.trash_talk: Datadog will nickel-and-dime you on every custom metric.

honeycomb.name: Honeycomb

review_excerpts: Honeycomb is purpose-built for high-cardinality observability. If you're doing distributed tracing at scale, nothing else comes close.
Smaller vendor with a passionate team. Support response times are excellent — real engineers, not scripts.
The query interface (BubbleUp) is genuinely innovative. You can find anomalies in minutes that would take hours in Datadog.
Limited ecosystem compared to Datadog. Some integrations require custom instrumentation. Fine for greenfield, harder for legacy stacks.
Pricing is predictable — events-based, not host-based. Much easier to budget for serverless and container workloads.

recent_incidents: Resolved - Oct 2023: Elevated query latency for ~20 min.

funding.name: Honeycomb
funding.notable: Still burning cash. Under pressure to reach profitability before next raise. May be flexible on pricing for logos.

ae_quote.competitor_dig: Splunk costs 10x for half the cardinality.

honeycomb.trash_talk: Splunk costs 10x for half the cardinality.

grafana.name: Grafana Cloud

review_excerpts: Open-source roots mean deep customization. Dashboards are beautiful and the community plugin library is huge.
Grafana Cloud is much simpler to run than self-hosted. Managed Loki and Tempo save real ops time.
No proprietary agent lock-in. We can switch backends without changing instrumentation — huge vendor negotiating leverage.
Alerting has improved but still feels like an afterthought vs Datadog. Complex routing rules are painful to set up.
Best price-to-value ratio in the category, especially for metrics. Logs and traces get expensive at scale.

recent_incidents: Resolved - Dec 2023: Loki log ingestion slow in eu-west-1 for ~45 min.

funding.name: Grafana Labs
funding.notable: Well-capitalized. Open-source flywheel drives low CAC. Can afford aggressive Cloud pricing to convert OSS users.

ae_quote.competitor_dig: You don't get locked in like with Datadog's proprietary agent.

grafana.trash_talk: You don't get locked in like with Datadog's proprietary agent.

splunk.name: Splunk

review_excerpts: Splunk is the gold standard for log analytics and SIEM. If compliance and security are your primary use case, nothing beats it.
Pricing is opaque and expensive. The per-GB ingestion model punishes verbose applications. Expect sticker shock.
SPL (Splunk Processing Language) is powerful but has a steep learning curve. Budget for training.
Cisco acquisition created uncertainty around roadmap. Several customers I know are actively evaluating alternatives.
Enterprise support is responsive and knowledgeable. Best support SLA of any vendor we've used.

recent_incidents: Resolved - Mar 2024: Search head cluster degraded, impacting saved searches for ~2h.
Resolved - Jan 2024: Splunk Cloud indexing delays in AWS us-east-1.

funding.name: Splunk
funding.notable: Cisco acquisition complete. Roadmap uncertainty is real. Enterprise customers are actively exploring alternatives. Strong negotiating angle for ShadowBuyer.

ae_quote.competitor_dig: Grafana is a hobby toy — we power Fortune 50 SOCs.

splunk.trash_talk: Grafana is a hobby toy — we power Fortune 50 SOCs.
